name: harvester
purpose: Erstes und meistgebautes Gebäude — extrahiert Energie aus dem Boden des Hex. Sitzt auf einer base_plate. Soll warm/freundlich wirken, klar als 'arbeitend' lesbar (animierter Rotor in Three.js).
version: v4 — palette-grey steel for clear contrast vs wood; rotor lifted on tall mast; wagon-wheel rotor with 12 wood blades + outer ring + small yellow hub

geometry.scale_factor_v3: 2x über v2 (Tower deutlich höher, Rotor visuell dominant)

HV_FoundationPad.embedded: 0.015 sinks into base_plate's deck — kein Float-Gap

HV_FoundationRim.shape: Top rim, r=0.70 (wider than post-corner distance 0.636 — posts visibly sit on it), h=0.025

HV_RotorSupport.shape: Tall vertical mast above platform: r=0.04, h=0.85 — lifts the rotor pivot to z=3.07 so the rotor's bottom blade clears the platform top (z=2.18)

HV_MastCap.shape: Disc on top of the mast, r=0.06, h=0.04 — where the axle attaches

HV_Posts.shape: 4 inward-tapered square posts (base 0.45, top 0.20 from center, height 1.30)
HV_Posts.material: mx_metal_steel (palette flat grey #9aa0a8 × VColor AO — KLAR silberner Stahl, eindeutig anders als die rost-texturierte Pipe und das Holz)
HV_Posts.rationale: v4-Wechsel: Polyhaven-Texturen für Stahl haben alle warme Untertöne (rusty_metal_*, factory_wall) und blieben braun/grün auch mit cool tints. Palette-Farbe garantiert echtes Silber-Grau.

HV_Braces.shape: 3 levels of X-pattern wooden cross-braces (12 X-shapes total = 24 diagonal struts)
HV_Braces.material: mx_wood_braces (wood_planks_dirt with darker tint — gealtertes Bauholz)
HV_Braces.rationale: User wollte Holz statt Metall für Braces — passt zum 'old wind-pump' Look

HV_TopPlatform.shape: Octagonal plate at the top of posts, r=0.32, h=0.05

HV_Rotor.shape: Classic wagon-wheel windpump rotor: axle (mx_metal_steel) + small yellow hub (mx_harvester_yellow) + 12 narrow wooden blades (mx_wood_rotor) + outer wooden ring (wagon-wheel binder) + yellow nose cone + rear nut
HV_Rotor.pivot: Origin set to (0, 0, 3.07) — the rotation center, above the new tall mast
HV_Rotor.anim_empty: anim_spin_x (PLAIN_AXES at pivot) — Three.js rotates HV_Rotor around X-axis

polyhaven_textures_used: rusty_metal_05 (1k → 512 diffuse) — Tower Posts, Foundation Pad/Rim, Bolts (cleaner silver metal, OTHER than pipe's rusty_metal_04)
wood_planks_dirt (1k → 512 diffuse) — Cross-Braces and Rotor Blades (with different tint multipliers)

material_tint_family.mx_wood_braces: (0.75, 0.65, 0.50) on wood_planks_dirt — aged wood for braces
material_tint_family.mx_wood_rotor: (0.95, 0.85, 0.70) on wood_planks_dirt — lighter wood for rotor blades
material_tint_family.mx_harvester_yellow: Palette #f59e0b × VColor — iconic yellow hub + nose cone

render_pipeline: Flat-shading + Cycles AO bake (32 samples) ins Vertex-Color Col → Texture × VColor MULTIPLY in Material. Three.js MeshBasicMaterial-tauglich.

techniques_applied: Posts (4) joined to single mesh, Braces (24) joined, Rotor (12 parts) joined

threejs_runtime_hints.load_position: Centered at hex world position (0,0,0). Building geometry from z=0.705 (just inside base_plate deck top) to z≈2.38.
threejs_runtime_hints.rotor_animation: scene.getObjectByName('HV_Rotor').rotation.x = t * 0.8  // ~0.8 rad/s spin like the legacy molanex spec
threejs_runtime_hints.anim_empty: anim_spin_x empty marks the pivot — useful as reference. The HV_Rotor's origin is already set to this pivot, so just rotation.x works.
threejs_runtime_hints.iconic_color: Yellow hub (#f59e0b) is the visual signature — visible from far. Three.js MeshBasicMaterial preserves it as a flat color.
